input_limitations: Figures are not visible (rendered as [FIGURE] placeholders), so model details are inferred from textual descriptions only.

models.model_label: Maritime ship domain OntoUML model (Figs. 2 and 6)

evidence_quotes.quote: An example of an OntoUML model in the shipping domain is shown in Fig. 2.
We now revisit the maritime ship example with the OntoUML diagram shown in Fig. 6.
Figure 5 presents the extended OntoUML profile, enabling the representation of high-order endurant types (we use the term 'classes' here following UML convention).

goals.justification: The maritime ship model is created and revisited explicitly to demonstrate the OntoUML modeling language constructs and the proposed extension. Per the Understanding vs. Learning disambiguation rule, work framed as a test case to learn whether/how modeling works is coded as For Learning, since the goal is to gain MODELING knowledge about OntoUML's stereotypes and constraints, not domain knowledge about ships.

evidence_quotes.quote: The OntoUML profile presented here is implemented as in a UML CASE tool which includes the automated verification of syntactical constraints in users' models.
These rules can be detected automatically in OntoUML models and rule out unsound models.

uncertain_goals.reason: The implementation refers to the OntoUML profile/plugin (the language tooling), not to the maritime ship model itself being implemented as a system or artifact; the ship model is only illustrative.
